metadata.name: Melee — Wings of the Guard gets +3/+3 attacking 3 distinct opponents in 4-player game
metadata.description: p1 controls Wings of the Guard (1/1 Flying Melee) and three other creatures. In a 4-player game, p1 declares Wings attacking p2, Llanowar Elves attacking p3, and Elvish Mystic attacking p4. CR 702.121a: Melee triggers when Wings attacks. When the trigger resolves, Wings gets +1/+1 for each opponent p1 attacked this combat (p2, p3, p4 — 3 distinct opponents), gaining +3/+3 until end of turn. Wings goes from 1/1 to 4/4. The scenario validates that Melee counts distinct opponents regardless of how many creatures attacked each opponent, and that attacking alongside creatures targeting different opponents scales the bonus correctly.
metadata.generation_notes: Wings of the Guard oracle text (MCP verified): {1}{W}, Creature — Bird, 1/1, Flying, Melee. CR 702.121a: 'Melee means: Whenever this creature attacks, it gets +1/+1 until end of turn for each opponent you attacked with a creature this combat.' Ruling 2016-08-23: 'You determine the size of the bonus as the melee ability resolves. Count each opponent that you attacked with one or more creatures. It doesn't matter if the attacking creatures are still attacking or even if they are still on the battlefield.' Ruling 2016-08-23: 'It doesn't matter how many creatures you attacked a player with, only that you attacked a player with at least one creature.' 4-player setup: p1 attacks p2 with Wings of the Guard, p3 with Llanowar Elves, p4 with Elvish Mystic. At trigger resolution time, p1 attacked 3 distinct opponents (p2, p3, p4), so Wings gets +3/+3 until end of turn → effective 4/4. Wings of the Guard is NOT in the Available Cards list and was verified via MCP lookup_card. Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic are in the Available Cards list as 1/1 creatures with a mana tap ability — they can attack freely (tapping them as attackers is the normal attack tap, separate from their mana ability which is also a tap). A fourth creature (Bog Raiders, 2/2 Swampwalk) is added to p1's side as the creature that does not attack, to make the battlefield realistic. Bog Raiders is listed in Available Cards. Turn 4 used to avoid summoning sickness (CR 302.6). P2, P3, P4 control no creatures so all attackers are unblocked. The Melee bonus is a 'until end of turn' continuous effect produced by the resolving trigger — it is NOT a +1/+1 counter. Assertion for Wings P/T as 4/4 at end of trigger resolution checks this correctly. CR 702.121b: only one instance of Melee here so no multi-instance interaction. Life totals unchanged — harness does not execute combat damage. The stack.count of 1 after declare_attackers confirms exactly one Melee trigger placed (Wings triggers, the other creatures have no attack triggers).

script.step_note: Turn 4 — p1 declares Wings of the Guard attacking p2, Llanowar Elves attacking p3, and Elvish Mystic attacking p4. Bog Raiders does not attack. CR 702.121a: Melee triggers on Wings of the Guard because it attacked. At trigger resolution, Wings gets +1/+1 for each of the 3 opponents p1 attacked this combat (p2, p3, p4) → +3/+3 → Wings becomes 4/4 until end of turn.

actions.description: Initial: Wings of the Guard, Llanowar Elves, Elvish Mystic, and Bog Raiders all untapped on p1 battlefield; all life totals 40; stack empty
actions.note: Turn 4 — all creatures have been controlled since before this turn; no summoning sickness (CR 302.6). P2, P3, and P4 control no creatures. Stack is empty before attack declaration.

CR 508.1: p1 declares Wings of the Guard attacking p2, Llanowar Elves attacking p3, and Elvish Mystic attacking p4. Bog Raiders is not declared as an attacker. CR 508.1f: All three declared attackers become tapped. CR 508.1k: All three become attacking creatures. CR 702.121a: Melee triggers on Wings of the Guard because it attacked this combat. The trigger is placed on the stack. Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic have no attack triggers.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Wings of the Guard, Llanowar Elves, and Elvish Mystic are tapped; Melee trigger on stack (count 1); Bog Raiders still untapped
actions.note: CR 508.1f: The three declared attackers tap simultaneously. CR 702.121a: Melee triggered ability for Wings of the Guard is placed on the stack. Stack count 1 confirms exactly one trigger fired (Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic have no attack triggers). Bog Raiders did not attack and remains untapped.
CR 116.3: All four players pass priority — the Melee triggered ability (top of stack) resolves.

actions.object: Wings of the Guard Melee trigger

resolution.note: CR 702.121a: Melee resolves. Count each opponent p1 attacked with at least one creature this combat: p2 (attacked with Wings of the Guard), p3 (attacked with Llanowar Elves), p4 (attacked with Elvish Mystic) — 3 distinct opponents. Wings of the Guard gets +3/+3 until end of turn. Base P/T 1/1 + 3/3 bonus = effective 4/4 until end of turn. Ruling 2016-08-23: bonus determined at resolution time; it does not matter if the other attacking creatures are still attacking.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Melee triggered ability resolves. Wings of the Guard gains +3/+3 until end of turn (3 opponents attacked). Effective P/T: 4/4 until end of turn.

actions.description: After Melee trigger resolves: stack empty; Wings of the Guard is 4/4 until end of turn; all three attackers remain tapped on battlefield; life totals unchanged
actions.note: Melee trigger has resolved. Wings of the Guard is now 4/4 until end of turn (base 1/1 + 3/3 until-end-of-turn bonus from Melee). Stack is empty. All four creatures remain on p1's battlefield. Life totals unchanged — harness does not execute combat damage. Ruling 2016-08-23: only distinct opponents counted (3: p2, p3, p4); it does not matter that only one creature attacked each. CR 702.121b: only one Melee trigger fires (Wings has a single Melee instance).
